english: primo, and primus. — 2. postp. c. genit. before, with respect to time Mil. — tóg-dráṅs-pa (thog drangs pa) Pth., Glr., Sch.: 'at first, begun'; our Lama explained it by 'to lead, to guide', v. tóg (thog) I, 4.
W. story (of a house); tóg-so (thog so) Mil. nt. id.

c. la, to strike, stumble, run against (like túg-pa (thug pa) v. 3); to be hindered, impeded, delayed, frq.; mi kyi gáṅ-laaṅ tógs-pa méd-du (mi kyi gang la'ang thogs pa med du) without being hindered by men, dogs, or anything else Mil.; tógs-pa-méd-pa (thogs pa med pa), togs-méd (thogs med), togs-brdúgs- (or (b)rtug-)méd-pa (thogs brdugs (or rtug) med pa), not hindered, unimpeded, unchecked; all-searching, all-penetrating.

1. Cs. a ploughman. — 2. Cs.: 'a ram that is castrated, wether; ra-toṅ (ra thong) a castrated he-goat'; according to my authorities, however, tóṅ-pa (thong pa), and ra-tóṅ (ra thong) signify a ram and he-goat one year old, toṅ-tsér (thong tser) and ra-tsér (ra tser) being the feminine forms (?) — 3. tóṅ-pai lo (thong pa'i lo) Mil. the years between childhood and manhood, juvenile years, Sch. tóṅ-po (thong po), cf. kyóg-toṅ (khyog thong).

1. Cs. a head-ornament, crown; gen. the usual covering for the head in the East, turban, la-tód (la thod) Glr. id.; dbu-tód (dbu thod) resp.; sá-yig tód-du bćíṅs-pai ka (sa yig thod du bcings pa'i ka) the letter k having for a crown the letter s: སྐ Zam. — 2. = tog (thog) I.: *go-tó'* (*sgo tho'*) C. over or above the door; k'a-tód-la (kha thod la), k'a-tóg-la (kha thog la), k'a-tód-la (kha thod la), up, upon Ld. — 3. threshold, yá-tod (ya thod), má-tod (ma thod) = yá-re (ya re), má-re (ma re). — 4. v. tód-pa (thod pa). — 5. tod-rgál ćé-ba (thod rgal ce ba) (toṅ? (thong?)) Mil., acc. to the context: angry, wrathful. — 6. tód-tód (thod thod) v. su.

1. skull, cranium; skull of a dead person, death's head; tod-skám (thod skam) a dry skull, tod-rlón (thod rlon) a fresh skull Thgr.; tod-k'rág (thod khrag) a skull filled with blood Thgr.; tod-pór (thod phor) a drinking-cup made of a skull. — 2. col. forehead, brow; tod-rtsá (thod rtsa) vena frontalis Lt.; tod-číṅs (thod cings), tod-kébs (thod khebs), tod-brgyán (thod brgyan), turban.
v. °tón-pa (thon pa) and °dón-pa (don pa); tón-pa (thon pa) C. also: good, fair, beautiful; smrá-bar tón-pa (smra bar thon pa) eloquent.
or tú-mi sam-bhó-ṭa (thu mi sam bho ta) n. of the minister that was sent to India by king Sroṅbtsansgampo, in order to procure an alphabet for writing.
Schr., *tob-če* (*thob ce*), tob-či (thob ci), teb-ču* (*theb cu*) C., button (v. tob-či (thob ci)).

I. vb. (synon. to rnyéd-pa (rnyed pa), and exclusively in use in W.) 1. to find, frq. — 2. to get, obtain, ṅas tob (ngas thob) B., ṅá-la tob (nga la thob) col., I find, I get; tób-par gyúr-ba (thob par gyur ba) id.; to partake of, to come to, dád-pa (dad pa) faith (to come to the faith) Mil.; to obtain, to get possession of, to subject to one's power Dzl.; da-drág tob-mtár (da drag thob mthar) Gram.: after (words) that have got a da-drág (da drag); saṅs-rgyás (sangs rgyas), rgyál-po (rgyal po), bdág-po (bdag po), tób-pa (thob pa) (lit. to get the Buddha etc.) to become a Buddha, a king, a lord; *čag-dzód tob-cè* (*phyag mdzod thob ce*) W. to become frq. (cf. rgyál-po (rgyal po)). — II. sbst. that which has been got or obtained: the sum, result, of a calculation etc. Wdn. III. *tób-če(s)* (*thob ces*) W. adj. that which is to be got or received, e.g. *búlon tób-čes-si bún-yig* (*bu lon thob ces si bun yig*) a list of demands to be called in, of money owing. Comp. tob-rgyál byéd-pa (thob rgyal byed pa) to rob, pillage, plunder (?) Sch.; tob-čá (thob cha) the share which one gets C. — tob-táṅ (thob thang) Cs. 'income, revenue'; more accurately: that which falls to one's share, as a reward or pay, for work, services etc., e.g. bits of cloth or silk, which a tailor may keep for himself. — tob-nór (thob nor) 1. share, quota. 2. quotient. — tob-bló (thob blo) C. desire, bkúr-sti tób-pa (bkur sti thob pa) ambition Schv. — tob-tsír (thob tshir) (lit. the turn of getting,
